phases.name: Foundation — Directive Coverage + File Compression
phases.description: Parallelizable mechanical work: add the passive /_shared/terse-output.md reference to every load-bearing file that lacks it, and run /blitz:compress on the remaining SAFE input files. No dependencies between the two workstreams; they can execute in the same sprint by different teammates.
exit_criteria: All 6 agent files reference terse-output
31 SKILL.md reference /_shared/terse-output.md
11 shared protocols cross-reference terse-output
7 SAFE reference.md + 12 research docs have .original backups
Structural validator passes

phases.name: Runtime Propagation — Write-Site Directive + Review Format
phases.description: Insert the 5-line inline output-style block at 8 SKILL.md write-phase locations. Inject the spawn-protocol §7 OUTPUT STYLE snippet into the 7 UNSAFE reference.md files' agent-prompt templates. Absorb the caveman-review line-level finding format into sprint-review and review.
entry_criteria: Phase 1 complete — terse-output is now referenced by every load-bearing file, so the runtime directive has a canonical target
exit_criteria: 8 SKILL.md contain the inline Output style block
7 UNSAFE reference.md contain OUTPUT STYLE snippet
sprint-review output uses L<line>: format with 🔴/🟡/🔵/❓ prefixes
Diff of sprint-review report vs prior sprint is reviewed and approved

phases.name: State + Gating — Intensity Persistence + LITE Markers + Dedup
phases.description: Extend developer-profile.json with output_intensity. Add LITE-intensity exemption markers to 9 safety/reasoning-sensitive skills. Add activity-feed message length rule. Extract agent-prompt boilerplate to shared fragment and refactor 7 UNSAFE reference.md to import. Boilerplate dedup runs last in this phase so the OUTPUT STYLE snippet injected in Phase 2 is preserved during the import refactor.
entry_criteria: Phase 2 complete — UNSAFE reference.md now carry the OUTPUT STYLE snippet, so the dedup refactor has stable content to extract around
exit_criteria: developer-profile.json schema documents output_intensity
BLITZ_OUTPUT_INTENSITY env override referenced in terse-output.md or spawn-protocol.md
9 skills carry LITE-intensity exemption markers
verbose-progress.md documents ≤200-char message rule
skills/_shared/agent-prompt-boilerplate.md exists
7 UNSAFE reference.md reference the shared fragment
Dumped-prompt parity test passes for one representative Agent() spawn
cf-task-type-gating transitions to status=dropped (superseded)

phases.name: Enforcement — WARNING → BLOCKER
phases.description: After Phase 3 lands and at least one full sprint ships clean, upgrade spawn-protocol.md:328 from WARNING to BLOCKER. Add Invariant 5 to sprint-review Phase 3.6. This phase intentionally gates behind a clean sprint to avoid trapping an in-flight sprint into failure.
entry_criteria: One full sprint post-Phase-3 shipped with zero WARNING-fires for the snippet check
exit_criteria: spawn-protocol.md no longer contains 'WARNING (not BLOCKER)'
sprint-review Phase 3.6 Invariant 5 documented and enforced
Sprint-review blocks any sprint with a missing snippet

phases.name: ui-audit Foundation — Skill Scaffold + Data Extraction + Consistency + Quality + Heuristics
phases.description: Build the ui-audit skill core: scaffold, page-data extraction, cross-page invariants, data-quality flags, UI/UX heuristic audit, and reporter. Delivers the baseline skill for single-role audits over an app's routes.
exit_criteria: skills/ui-audit/ runs end-to-end on a sample app
docs/crawls/page-data-registry.jsonl + ui-audit-report.md produced
Invariants + quality + heuristic findings verified on fixture

phases.name: ui-audit Coverage Expansion — Buttons + Events + Role Matrix
phases.description: Extend ui-audit from numeric+heuristic coverage to full UX + role-boundary coverage. Three parallel workstreams build on the Phase 5 core.
exit_criteria: buttons/events/role modes implemented
Full smoke run (anonymous+admin) passes on fixture app
Role-leak detection demonstrated catching a seeded leak

critical_path: CAP-006 (depends on CAP-003, runs in Phase 3 after Phase 2 lands)
notes: CAP-007 is intentionally deferred to a separate phase with a sprint-gap to avoid trapping an in-flight sprint. If sprint cadence is weekly, CAP-007 lands ~2 weeks after CAP-003.

Phase 1 and the Phase 2 write-phase-directive workstream edit the same SKILL.md files. Run Phase 1's SKILL.md edits first, then Phase 2's write-phase inserts — easy serial work, same commit batch acceptable.
